    RuntimePointerChecking &RtCheck, ScalarEvolution *SE, Loop *TheLoop,
    const DenseMap<Value *, const SCEV *> &StridesMap,
    Value *&UncomputablePtr) {

  // to place a runtime bound check.
  bool CanDoRT = true;

  bool MayNeedRTCheck = false;
  if (!IsRTCheckAnalysisNeeded) return true;

  bool IsDepCheckNeeded = isDependencyCheckNeeded();

  // We assign a consecutive id to access from different alias sets.
  // Accesses between different groups doesn't need to be checked.
  unsigned ASId = 0;
  for (const auto &AS : AST) {
    int NumReadPtrChecks = 0;
    int NumWritePtrChecks = 0;
    bool CanDoAliasSetRT = true;
    ++ASId;
    auto ASPointers = AS.getPointers();

    // We assign consecutive id to access from different dependence sets.
    // Accesses within the same set don't need a runtime check.
    unsigned RunningDepId = 1;

    // collect MemAccessInfos for later.
    SmallVector<MemAccessInfo, 4> AccessInfos;
    for (const Value *ConstPtr : ASPointers) {

      if (IsWrite)

      else

      AccessInfos.emplace_back(Ptr, IsWrite);

bool RuntimePointerChecking::arePointersInSamePartition(
    const SmallVectorImpl<int> &PtrToPartition, unsigned PtrIdx1,

}

bool RuntimePointerChecking::needsChecking(unsigned I, unsigned J) const {
  const PointerInfo &PointerI = Pointers[I];
  const PointerInfo &PointerJ = Pointers[J];

  // No need to check if two readonly pointers intersect.
  if (!PointerI.IsWritePtr && !PointerJ.IsWritePtr)
    return false;

  // Only need to check pointers between two different dependency sets.
  if (PointerI.DependencySetId == PointerJ.DependencySetId)
    return false;

  // Only need to check pointers in the same alias set.
  return PointerI.AliasSetId == PointerJ.AliasSetId;
}

bool RuntimePointerChecking::arePointersInSamePartition(
    const SmallVectorImpl<int> &PtrToPartition, unsigned PtrIdx1,
    unsigned PtrIdx2) {
  return (PtrToPartition[PtrIdx1] != -1 &&
          PtrToPartition[PtrIdx1] == PtrToPartition[PtrIdx2]);
}

bool RuntimePointerChecking::needsChecking(unsigned I, unsigned J) const {
  const PointerInfo &PointerI = Pointers[I];
  const PointerInfo &PointerJ = Pointers[J];

  // No need to check if two readonly pointers intersect.
  if (!PointerI.IsWritePtr && !PointerJ.IsWritePtr)
    return false;

  // Only need to check pointers between two different dependency sets.
  if (PointerI.DependencySetId == PointerJ.DependencySetId)
    return false;

  // Only need to check pointers in the same alias set.
  return PointerI.AliasSetId == PointerJ.AliasSetId;
}

void RuntimePointerChecking::printChecks(
    raw_ostream &OS, const SmallVectorImpl<RuntimePointerCheck> &Checks,
    unsigned Depth) const {
  unsigned N = 0;
  for (const auto &[Check1, Check2] : Checks) {
    const auto &First = Check1->Members, &Second = Check2->Members;

    OS.indent(Depth) << "Check " << N++ << ":\n";

    OS.indent(Depth + 2) << "Comparing group (" << Check1 << "):\n";
    for (unsigned K : First)
      OS.indent(Depth + 2) << *Pointers[K].PointerValue << "\n";

    OS.indent(Depth + 2) << "Against group (" << Check2 << "):\n";
    for (unsigned K : Second)
      OS.indent(Depth + 2) << *Pointers[K].PointerValue << "\n";
  }
}

To ensure this happens for mixed address spaces within an alias set, I will insert a check after collecting AccessInfos and AliasSetAddressSpaces but before the "We do not need runtime checks for this alias set" logic. This check will mark CanDoAliasSetRT and CanDoRT as false and set UncomputablePtr, so canCheckPtrAtRT returns false, triggering the expected report.

```bash
awk 'BEGIN{inserted=0} {print} /We do not need runtime checks for this alias set/{if(!inserted){print "    // Mixed address spaces within this alias set cannot be reliably checked at runtime."; print "    if (AliasSetAddressSpaces.size() > 1) {"; print "      CanDoAliasSetRT = false;"; print "      CanDoRT = false;"; print "      if (!AccessInfos.empty())"; print "        UncomputablePtr = AccessInfos.front().getPointer();"; print "    }"; inserted=1}}' llvm/lib/Analysis/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp > /tmp/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp && mv /tmp/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp llvm/lib/Analysis/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp && submit-patch
```
